Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. I'll provide complete context from my exploration.

## Project Structure

Monorepo with Bun, Turborepo:
- `packages/shared` — shared utilities, subpath exports (no barrel), plain TypeScript, no build step (src files are the published interface)
- `packages/contracts` — Effect/Schema types, not relevant here
- `apps/web` — React 19 + Vite + Tailwind CSS v4 + TanStack Router + Zustand

**Constraint**: Do NOT modify build configuration or add new dependencies.

## Existing Theme System (to be replaced/extended)

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** (106 lines):
```typescript
type Theme = "light" | "dark" | "system";
const STORAGE_KEY = "t3code:theme";
// Uses useSyncExternalStore (external store pattern, no React context)
// On module load: applyTheme(getStored()) -- prevents flash
// applyTheme(): only toggles .dark class on document.documentElement
// useTheme() returns { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark" }
```

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** (407 lines):
- Tailwind v4 `@theme inline` block maps `--color-*` to CSS custom properties
- `:root` defines `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--card`, `--popover`, `--muted`, `--secondary`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, semantic colors
- `@variant dark { ... }` inside `:root` overrides vars for dark mode
- Key: muted/secondary/accent/border/input use `--alpha(var(--color-black) / N%)` which is a Tailwind v4 CSS macro (NOT usable from JavaScript)
- `--card` and `--popover` use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, white)` — these automatically pick up JS-overridden `--background`

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** (609 lines):
- Has an "Appearance" section with THEME_OPTIONS = [{value: "system"|"light"|"dark", label, description}]
- Renders as a simple radio-button-like list of clickable rows
- Calls `useTheme()` and `setTheme(option.value)`

**`apps/web/src/main.tsx`**:
```tsx
ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </React.StrictMode>,
);
```

**`packages/shared/package.json`** (subpath exports):
```json
"exports": {
  "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" },
  "./git": { ... },
  "./logging": { ... },
  "./shell": { ... },
  "./Net": { ... }
}
```

## Requirements

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`**: Define `ThemeName = "t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"`, `ThemeColors` (background, foreground, accent, accentForeground, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar), `ThemeDefinition` (name, label, description, isDark, colors), `BUILT_IN_THEMES` record for all 3 concrete themes. No external deps.

2. **`packages/shared/package.json`**: Add `"./theme"` subpath export.

3. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`**: Refactor to use `ThemeName` from shared. New storage key `t3code:custom-theme`. Migrate old `t3code:theme` values (light→t3-light, dark→t3-dark, system→system). `applyTheme()` must set `.dark` class AND inject CSS custom properties via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()`. Only override: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring`, plus new `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar`. Let existing CSS `--alpha()` and `color-mix()` derivations continue working via the `.dark` class.

4. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`**: React component that wraps children, calls `useTheme()`, uses `useEffect` to re-apply CSS properties on theme change (React StrictMode safe). Exports `ThemeProvider`.

5. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`**: Visual theme picker with preview swatches. For each concrete theme (t3-light, t3-dark, t3-midnight): small mini preview box showing background, sidebar strip, accent dot, status bar strip — all using inline styles with the theme's actual color values (NOT CSS vars, so the preview shows correct colors regardless of current theme). For "system": a split half-light/half-dark preview. Each option shows label, description. Selected option has a highlight. Uses `useTheme()` hook. Follows the existing component patterns (Tailwind classes, no deps).

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`**: Remove the `THEME_OPTIONS` array and the radio button list in the Appearance section. Replace with `<ThemeSelector />`. Keep `resolvedTheme` display text.

7. **`apps/web/src/main.tsx`**: Wrap `RouterProvider` in `ThemeProvider`.

## Key Design Decisions to Include in Plan

- The `--alpha()` Tailwind macro in `index.css` resolves at CSS parse time and can't be reproduced in JS. Solution: DON'T override `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input`. These use black/white alpha based on `.dark` class, which we DO control. So they adapt automatically.
- `--card` and `--popover` use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` which auto-updates when `--background` is overridden via JS.
- The 4 new `--theme-*` variables don't need to be in `@theme inline` unless Tailwind utilities reference them. They can just be CSS custom properties set via JS and consumed with `var(--theme-sidebar)` inline styles or `[var(--theme-sidebar)]` Tailwind syntax.

## Theme Color Values to Define

**t3-light**: 
- background: `#ffffff`, foreground: `#262626`, accent: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, accentForeground: `#ffffff`
- sidebar: `#f5f5f5`, editor: `#ffffff`, terminal: `#fafafa`, statusBar: `#ebebeb`
- isDark: false

**t3-dark** (matches current dark mode):
- background: `color-mix(in srgb, #030712 95%, white)`, foreground: `#f5f5f5`, accent: `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, accentForeground: `#ffffff`
- sidebar: `color-mix(in srgb, #030712 97%, white)`, editor: `#0a0a0a`, terminal: `#0a0a0a`, statusBar: `#0d0d0d`
- isDark: true

**t3-midnight** (deep blue-black, more saturated):
- background: `oklch(0.10 0.025 264)`, foreground: `#e8eaf6`, accent: `oklch(0.65 0.25 280)`, accentForeground: `#ffffff`
- sidebar: `oklch(0.09 0.03 264)`, editor: `oklch(0.08 0.02 264)`, terminal: `oklch(0.07 0.02 264)`, statusBar: `oklch(0.09 0.03 264)`
- isDark: true

## Output Required

Provide:
1. The exact implementation steps in order
2. Critical code snippets for each file
3. What to verify after implementation (lint, typecheck, manual testing)
4. Any TypeScript strictness considerations (exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, etc.)

# Custom Theme System for T3 Code Editor ## Context The app currently has a minimal two-state theme system (`"light" | "dark" | "system"`) stored at `t3code:theme` that only toggles a `.dark` class. The task is to introduce a fully-featured named theme system: three built-in themes with defined color tokens (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, status bar), a React ThemeProvider, an updated `useTheme` hook that applies CSS custom properties, and a visual ThemeSelector with preview swatches. Constraints: no build-config changes, no new dependencies. --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | Types + built-in theme data (no deps) | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | React wrapper component | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Visual swatch picker | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./theme"` subpath export | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Refactor for new ThemeName type + CSS var injection | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace radio list with `` | | `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | Wrap `RouterProvider` in `ThemeProvider` | --- ## Step-by-Step Implementation ### Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (new file) Pure TypeScript, zero external dependencies. ```typescript export type ThemeName = "t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"; export type ConcreteThemeName = Exclude; export interface ThemeColors { background: string; foreground: string; accent: string; accentForeground: string; sidebar: string; editor: string; terminal: string; statusBar: string; } export interface ThemeDefinition { name: ConcreteThemeName; label: string; description: string; isDark: boolean; colors: ThemeColors; } export const BUILT_IN_THEMES: Record = { "t3-light": { name: "t3-light", label: "Light", description: "Clean light theme with T3's signature purple.", isDark: false, colors: { background: "#ffffff", foreground: "#262626", accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "#f5f5f5", editor: "#ffffff", terminal: "#fafafa", statusBar: "#ebebeb", }, }, "t3-dark": { name: "t3-dark", label: "Dark", description: "Rich dark theme, easy on the eyes.", isDark: true, colors: { background: "color-mix(in srgb, #030712 95%, white)", foreground: "#f5f5f5", accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "color-mix(in srgb, #030712 97%, white)", editor: "#0a0a0a", terminal: "#0a0a0a", statusBar: "#0d0d0d", }, }, "t3-midnight": { name: "t3-midnight", label: "Midnight", description: "Deep blue-black with vibrant accents.", isDark: true, colors: { background: "oklch(0.10 0.025 264)", foreground: "#e8eaf6", accent: "oklch(0.65 0.25 280)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "oklch(0.09 0.03 264)", editor: "oklch(0.08 0.02 264)", terminal: "oklch(0.07 0.02 264)", statusBar: "oklch(0.09 0.03 264)", }, }, }; export const CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES: ReadonlyArray = [ "t3-light", "t3-dark", "t3-midnight", ]; ``` ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add one entry matching the existing pattern (types + import both point to the src file): ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (full rewrite) Key changes from the original 106-line file: - Import `ThemeName`, `ConcreteThemeName`, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` from `@t3tools/shared/theme` - New storage key: `"t3code:custom-theme"` (old: `"t3code:theme"`) - Add `migrateTheme()` that reads the new key first, then falls back to mapping the old key (`"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` / missing → `"system"`) - Replace `getStored()` with `migrateTheme()` everywhere - Update `applyTheme(theme: ThemeName)`: 1. Resolve concrete name (`system` → `t3-dark` or `t3-light` based on `getSystemDark()`) 2. Toggle `.dark` class from `ThemeDefinition.isDark` 3. Call `applyThemeCssVars(def.colors)` — sets the following CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement.style` using `setProperty`: - `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring` - `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar` 4. Keep the `no-transitions` / reflow / rAF suppression logic unchanged - **Do NOT** set `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` — these use Tailwind v4's `--alpha()` macro (build-time only) and auto-adapt via the `.dark` class - `--card` and `--popover` auto-update because their CSS definitions reference `var(--background)` - Preserve `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` return type (backward-compatible with any consumers that pass it to diff-rendering utilities): `BUILT_IN_THEMES[concreteName]?.isDark ? "dark" : "light"` - Storage event handler checks both `STORAGE_KEY` and the old key for cross-tab compatibility - The module-level `applyTheme(migrateTheme())` call on load is preserved for flash prevention `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` note: `BUILT_IN_THEMES[name]` infers `ThemeDefinition | undefined`. Guard with `if (def === undefined) return` in `applyTheme`, or use `BUILT_IN_THEMES[name] as ThemeDefinition` where the key is a `ConcreteThemeName` literal (following the existing pattern in `shared/src/model.ts` which uses `as` casts for known-exhaustive records). ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (new file) Thin component. Subscribes to theme changes so the internal `useEffect` in `useTheme` runs from the top of the tree (important for StrictMode safety and SSR hydration order): ```tsx import { type ReactNode } from "react"; import { useTheme } from "~/hooks/useTheme"; export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children?: ReactNode }) { // Subscribing here ensures useTheme's internal useEffect fires at the root, // keeping CSS vars and .dark in sync through React's lifecycle (StrictMode safe). useTheme(); return <>{children}; } ``` The original `useTheme` already contains `useEffect(() => { applyTheme(theme); }, [theme])`. Mounting `ThemeProvider` at the root means this effect runs before any child renders needing the theme. `applyTheme` is idempotent so StrictMode double-invocation is harmless. ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) Renders one button per theme (3 concrete + 1 system). Preview boxes use **inline styles with hard-coded color values** from `BUILT_IN_THEMES` — NOT `var(--*)` — so swatches show correct colors independent of the currently active theme. Preview swatch anatomy (64×40px box): - Full-width background fill - Left strip ~12px: sidebar color - Bottom strip ~6px: statusBar color - Small circle top-right: accent color System swatch: left half uses t3-light background, right half uses t3-dark background. Follows existing styling patterns from `_chat.settings.tsx`: - Selected state: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` + "Active" badge (`bg-primary/14 text-primary`) - Unselected: `border-border bg-background hover:bg-accent text-muted-foreground` - `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` for accessibility - Uses `cn()` from `~/lib/utils` Import chain: `@t3tools/shared/theme` (BUILT_IN_THEMES, CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES, ThemeName), `~/hooks/useTheme`, `~/lib/utils` (cn). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant (lines ~25–41) - Remove `theme` and `setTheme` from `useTheme()` destructure (only `resolvedTheme` stays) - Remove the `
…
` block (~lines 210–238) - Add import: `import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector";` - Replace removed block with `` - Keep the "Active theme: {resolvedTheme}" paragraph unchanged ### Step 7 — `apps/web/src/main.tsx` ```tsx import { ThemeProvider } from "./components/ThemeProvider"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( , ); ``` --- ## Architecture Notes **Why only 5 CSS vars are overridden (not all 20):** `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` use `--alpha(var(--color-black) / N%)` which is a Tailwind v4 build-time CSS macro — it cannot be reproduced via `setProperty`. These variables auto-adapt to dark/light via the `.dark` class, which we control. `--card` and `--popover` use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` and cascade automatically once `--background` is overridden from JS. **Why `resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"`:** This return type is relied upon by diff-rendering logic (e.g. any component passing `resolvedTheme` to `@pierre/diffs` theme configuration). Keeping it `"light" | "dark"` avoids breaking changes. **Why no `@theme inline` additions in `index.css`:** The 4 new `--theme-*` variables are set entirely from JS. They are usable anywhere via `var(--theme-sidebar)` in inline styles or Tailwind's arbitrary-value syntax `[var(--theme-sidebar)]` without being registered in `@theme inline`. No CSS file changes needed. --- ## Verification ```bash # Must both pass before done bun typecheck # from packages/shared and apps/web bun lint # from apps/web ``` **Manual testing checklist:** 1. Fresh load → no theme flash; correct theme applied 2. Switch to each of the 3 concrete themes → `document.documentElement.style` has correct `--background`, `--primary`, `--theme-sidebar`, etc.; `.dark` class present/absent correctly 3. Switch to "system" → follows OS preference; switching OS preference updates theme live 4. Reload page → selected theme persists 5. Migration: set `localStorage["t3code:theme"] = "dark"`, clear `t3code:custom-theme`, reload → migrates to `t3-dark` automatically 6. Open two tabs → changing theme in one tab updates the other (storage event) 7. ThemeSelector swatches → each swatch shows its own colors even when a different theme is active 8. `resolvedTheme` text in Settings → shows "light" or "dark" correctly 9. `--card` / `--popover` → verify they track `--background` changes (inspect in DevTools) 10. `--secondary` / `--border` → verify they are NOT overridden by JS (only `.dark` class affects them)

Preview swatch anatomy (64×40px box): - Full-width background fill - Left strip ~12px: sidebar color - Bottom strip ~6px: statusBar color - Small circle top-right: accent color System swatch: left half uses t3-light background, right half uses t3-dark background. Follows existing styling patterns from `_chat.settings.tsx`: - Selected state: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` + "Active" badge (`bg-primary/14 text-primary`) - Unselected: `border-border bg-background hover:bg-accent text-muted-foreground` - `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` for accessibility - Uses `cn()` from `~/lib/utils` Import chain: `@t3tools/shared/theme` (BUILT_IN_THEMES, CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES, ThemeName), `~/hooks/useTheme`, `~/lib/utils` (cn). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant (lines ~25–41) - Remove `theme` and `setTheme` from `useTheme()` destructure (only `resolvedTheme` stays) - Remove the `
…
` block (~lines 210–238) - Add import: `import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector";` - Replace removed block with `` - Keep the "Active theme: {resolvedTheme}" paragraph unchanged ### Step 7 — `apps/web/src/main.tsx` ```tsx import { ThemeProvider } from "./components/ThemeProvider"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( , ); ``` --- ## Architecture Notes **Why only 5 CSS vars are overridden (not all 20):** `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` use `--alpha(var(--color-black) / N%)` which is a Tailwind v4 build-time CSS macro — it cannot be reproduced via `setProperty`. These variables auto-adapt to dark/light via the `.dark` class, which we control. `--card` and `--popover` use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` and cascade automatically once `--background` is overridden from JS. **Why `resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"`:** This return type is relied upon by diff-rendering logic (e.g. any component passing `resolvedTheme` to `@pierre/diffs` theme configuration). Keeping it `"light" | "dark"` avoids breaking changes. **Why no `@theme inline` additions in `index.css`:** The 4 new `--theme-*` variables are set entirely from JS. They are usable anywhere via `var(--theme-sidebar)` in inline styles or Tailwind's arbitrary-value syntax `[var(--theme-sidebar)]` without being registered in `@theme inline`. No CSS file changes needed. --- ## Verification ```bash # Must both pass before done bun typecheck # from packages/shared and apps/web bun lint # from apps/web ``` **Manual testing checklist:** 1. Fresh load → no theme flash; correct theme applied 2. Switch to each of the 3 concrete themes → `document.documentElement.style` has correct `--background`, `--primary`, `--theme-sidebar`, etc.; `.dark` class present/absent correctly 3. Switch to "system" → follows OS preference; switching OS preference updates theme live 4. Reload page → selected theme persists 5. Migration: set `localStorage["t3code:theme"] = "dark"`, clear `t3code:custom-theme`, reload → migrates to `t3-dark` automatically 6. Open two tabs → changing theme in one tab updates the other (storage event) 7. ThemeSelector swatches → each swatch shows its own colors even when a different theme is active 8. `resolvedTheme` text in Settings → shows "light" or "dark" correctly 9. `--card` / `--popover` → verify they track `--background` changes (inspect in DevTools) 10. `--secondary` / `--border` → verify they are NOT overridden by JS (only `.dark` class affects them)

content.text:
Here's the full implementation plan:

---

## Plan: Custom Theme System

### 7 files touched (3 new, 4 modified), no new dependencies

#### New files
| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | Types + built-in theme data — pure TS, zero deps |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | Thin React root wrapper |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Visual swatch picker |

#### Modified files
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./theme"` subpath export (same pattern as `"./model"`) |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Full rewrite — new ThemeName type, CSS var injection |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Swap radio-button list for `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | Wrap `RouterProvider` in `ThemeProvider` |

---

### Key design decisions

**Theme types (`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`):**
- `ThemeName = "t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"`
- `ThemeColors` covers all 7 areas: background, foreground, accent, accentForeground, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar
- `BUILT_IN_THEMES: Record<ConcreteThemeName, ThemeDefinition>` with all three themes defined
- t3-midnight uses `oklch(0.10 0.025 264)` background with `oklch(0.65 0.25 280)` accent

**Why only 5 CSS vars are set from JavaScript, not all 20:**
`--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` use Tailwind v4's `--alpha()` build-time macro and can't be reproduced in JS. They auto-adapt via the `.dark` class. `--card`/`--popover` auto-cascade because they use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, …)` — overriding `--background` updates them for free.

The 5 vars set from JS: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring` + 4 new semantic vars: `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar`.

**Storage migration:** New key is `t3code:custom-theme`. `migrateTheme()` reads the new key first; if absent, maps the old `t3code:theme` value (`dark`→`t3-dark`, `light`→`t3-light`, `system`→`system`).

**`resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"`** for backward compatibility with diff-rendering utilities that consume it.

**`ThemeSelector` swatches use inline styles with hard-coded color values** (not CSS vars), so they show the correct colors regardless of which theme is currently active.

**No `@theme inline` changes** needed — the new `--theme-*` vars are consumed via `var(--theme-sidebar)` inline or Tailwind's arbitrary syntax, no registration required.

**Verification:** `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` in both `packages/shared` and `apps/web`, plus a 10-point manual checklist (flash prevention, persistence, migration, cross-tab sync, swatch preview correctness).

cat > /tmp/t3code_project_exploration.md << 'EOF'
# T3 Code Project Structure Exploration

## 1. Monorepo Structure & Package.json Setup

### Root `/package.json`
- **Package Manager**: Bun v1.3.9 (configured as packageManager)
- **Node**: v24.13.1
- **Type**: "module" (ESM)
- **Workspaces**: Turborepo-based with:
  - `apps/*` (web, desktop, server, marketing)
  - `packages/*` (contracts, shared)
  - `scripts`
- **Build Tool**: Turbo (v2.3.3) for monorepo task orchestration
- **Linting**: oxlint (v1.50.0) + oxfmt (v0.35.0) - Rust-based tooling
- **Testing**: vitest
- **Catalog**: Shared dependency versions for workspace packages
  - effect, typescript, vitest pinned across packages
  - Custom Effect TypeScript LSP plugin setup

### Key Dependencies
- **Build**: Vite 8 beta with Tailwind CSS v4 vite plugin
- **Type System**: TypeScript 5.7.3 with strict mode enabled

---

## 2. `packages/shared` - Shared Utilities

### Structure & Exports (`package.json`)
Located at `/packages/shared/package.json`

**Exported modules** (ES modules, direct source):
- `./model` → `src/model.ts` - Model utilities
- `./git` → `src/git.ts` - Git utilities
- `./logging` → `src/logging.ts` - Logging utilities
- `./shell` → `src/shell.ts` - Shell utilities
- `./Net` → `src/Net.ts` - Network utilities

### Module Details

#### `model.ts` - Model Resolution & Options
**Functions**:
- `getModelOptions(provider)` - Get available models for a provider
- `normalizeModelSlug(model, provider)` - Normalize user input to valid model slug
- `resolveModelSlug(model, provider)` - Resolve to valid model or use default
- `resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider, model)` - Explicit provider resolver
- `getReasoningEffortOptions(provider)` - Get reasoning effort options
- `getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider)` - Get default reasoning effort
- `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS` - ["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]

**Dependencies**: Uses contracts package for model definitions (MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER, DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER, etc.)

#### `git.ts` - Branch Name Sanitization
**Functions**:
- `sanitizeBranchFragment(raw)` - Converts arbitrary string to valid git branch fragment
  - Strips quotes, collapses separators, limits to 64 chars
  - Lowercases, removes special chars (replaces with hyphens)
- `sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw)` - Creates `feature/…` branch names
- `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(existingBranchNames, preferredBranch)` - Auto-generates unique branch names with numeric suffixes

#### `shell.ts` - Shell Environment Utilities
**Functions**:
- `readPathFromLoginShell(shell, execFile)` - Extracts PATH from login shell
- `extractPathFromShellOutput(output)` - Helper to parse shell output

#### `Net.ts` - Network Service (Effect-based)
**Export**: NetService - Effect-based service for network operations
**Methods**:
- `canListenOnHost(port, host)` - Check if TCP server can bind to host:port
- `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)` - Check IPv4 + IPv6 loopback availability
- `reserveLoopbackPort(host?)` - Reserve ephemeral loopback port
- `findAvailablePort(preferred)` - Find available port (prefers given port first)

**Error**: NetError (Tagged error with message and cause)

#### `logging.ts` - Rotating File Logger
**Export**: RotatingFileSink class
**Constructor options**:
- `filePath`: string
- `maxBytes`: number (minimum 1)
- `maxFiles`: number (minimum 1)
- `throwOnError?`: boolean

**Methods**:
- `write(chunk)` - Write string or Buffer to log file with automatic rotation

---

## 3. `packages/contracts` - Type Contracts & Schemas

### Structure (`package.json`)
- **Type**: module
- **Main/Module exports**: Dual CJS/ESM via tsdown build tool
- **Types**: Direct source import from `src/index.ts`
- **Build**: tsdown (Rust bundler) produces dist/index.cjs and dist/index.mjs
- **Build script**: `tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --clean`

### Exported Modules (`src/index.ts`)
Exports from:
- `baseSchemas.ts` - Core branded types
- `ipc.ts` - IPC contracts
- `terminal.ts` - Terminal contracts
- `provider.ts` - Provider contracts
- `providerRuntime.ts` - Runtime contracts
- `model.ts` - Model definitions
- `ws.ts` - WebSocket contracts
- `keybindings.ts` - Keybinding contracts
- `server.ts` - Server contracts
- `git.ts` - Git contracts
- `orchestration.ts` - Orchestration contracts
- `editor.ts` - Editor contracts
- `project.ts` - Project contracts

### Key Schemas

#### `baseSchemas.ts` - Foundation Types
**Utility Schemas**:
- `TrimmedString` - String with trim validation
- `TrimmedNonEmptyString` - Non-empty trimmed strings
- `NonNegativeInt` - Integers >= 0
- `PositiveInt` - Integers >= 1
- `IsoDateTime` - ISO date-time string

**Branded IDs** (enforced non-empty trimmed strings):
- `ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, `CommandId`, `EventId`, `MessageId`, `TurnId`
- `ProviderItemId`, `RuntimeSessionId`, `RuntimeItemId`, `RuntimeRequestId`, `RuntimeTaskId`
- `ApprovalRequestId`, `CheckpointRef`

#### `model.ts` - Model Configuration
**Reasoning Effort**:
- `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS = ["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]`

**Model Options**:
```
MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER.codex = [
  { slug: "gpt-5.4", name: "GPT-5.4" },
  { slug: "gpt-5.3-codex", name: "GPT-5.3 Codex" },
  { slug: "gpt-5.3-codex-spark", name: "GPT-5.3 Codex Spark" },
  { slug: "gpt-5.2-codex", name: "GPT-5.2 Codex" },
  { slug: "gpt-5.2", name: "GPT-5.2" },
]
```

**Default**: `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER.codex = "gpt-5.4"`

**Model Slug Aliases**:
- "5.4" → "gpt-5.4"
- "5.3" → "gpt-5.3-codex"
- "5.3-spark" → "gpt-5.3-codex-spark"

#### `orchestration.ts` - Orchestration Contracts
**Provider Types**:
- `ProviderKind = "codex"` (currently only codex supported)
- `ProviderApprovalPolicy = "untrusted" | "on-failure" | "on-request" | "never"`
- `ProviderSandboxMode = "read-only" | "workspace-write" | "danger-full-access"`
- `ProviderServiceTier = "fast" | "flex"`
- `RuntimeMode = "approval-required" | "full-access"`
- `ProviderInteractionMode = "default" | "plan"`
- `ProviderRequestKind = "command" | "file-read" | "file-change"`
- `AssistantDeliveryMode = "buffered" | "streaming"`

**WebSocket Methods** (ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS):
- `getSnapshot` - Get current state
- `dispatchCommand` - Send command
- `getTurnDiff` - Get turn diff
- `getFullThreadDiff` - Get full thread diff
- `replayEvents` - Replay events

**Chat Attachment Types**:
- `ChatImageAttachment` - Image type with size limits
- Max input: 120,000 chars
- Max attachments: 8
- Max image: 10MB

---

## 4. `apps/web` - React Web Application

### Package Configuration (`package.json`)
- **Name**: @t3tools/web
- **Type**: module
- **Build Tool**: Vite 8.0 beta
- **Framework**: React 19
- **Router**: TanStack Router v1.160+
- **State Management**: Zustand v5 (stores)
- **UI Components**: Base UI + custom component library

### Key Dependencies
**Core**:
- `react` & `react-dom` v19
- `@tanstack/react-router` v1.160.2
- `@tanstack/react-query` v5.90.0

**UI/Styling**:
- `@tailwindcss/vite` v4.0.0 (Vite plugin)
- `tailwindcss` v4.0.0
- `@base-ui/react` v1.2.0 (Headless UI)
- `class-variance-authority` v0.7.1 (CVA for component variants)
- `tailwind-merge` v3.4.0 (Tailwind class merging)
- `lucide-react` v0.564.0 (Icons)

**Editor/Content**:
- `@lexical/react` v0.41.0 + `lexical` v0.41.0 (Rich text editor)
- `react-markdown` v10.1.0
- `remark-gfm` v4.0.1 (GitHub Flavored Markdown)

**Terminal/UI**:
- `@xterm/xterm` v6.0.0 + `@xterm/addon-fit`
- `@tanstack/react-virtual` v3.13.18 (Virtualization)
- `@tanstack/react-pacer` v0.19.4

**Diffs/Visualization**:
- `@pierre/diffs` v1.1.0-beta.16 (Diff rendering)

**Build/Dev**:
- `vite` v8.0.0-beta.12
- `@vitejs/plugin-react` v5.1.4
- `@tanstack/router-plugin` v1.161.0 (Router codegen)
- `babel-plugin-react-compiler` v19.0.0-beta (React compiler)
- `msw` v2.12.10 (API mocking)
- `typescript` catalog: v5.7.3
- `vitest` (testing)

### TypeScript Config (`apps/web/tsconfig.json`)
**Extends**: `../../tsconfig.base.json`

**Options**:
- `jsx`: "react-jsx"
- `lib`: ["ES2023", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]
- `target`: ES2023 (from base)
- **Paths**:
  - `~/*` → `./src/*` (alias for imports)
- **Plugin**: @effect/language-service with strict diagnostics
  - importFromBarrel: error
  - anyUnknownInErrorContext: warning

### Vite Config (`apps/web/vite.config.ts`)
**Plugins**:
1. TanStack Router plugin (for file-based routing)
2. React plugin with React Compiler (babel plugin)
3. Tailwind CSS Vite plugin

**Server Config**:
- **Port**: 5733 (configurable via PORT env var)
- **HMR**: Explicit WebSocket config for Electron compatibility
  - protocol: "ws"
  - host: "localhost"

**Build**:
- **Output**: dist/
- **Sourcemap**: Configurable via T3CODE_WEB_SOURCEMAP env var
  - "0" or "false" → no sourcemap
  - "hidden" → hidden sourcemap
  - default → inline sourcemap

**Optimizations**:
- `@pierre/diffs` dependencies pre-optimized
- Native plugins enabled (experimental)
- TSConfig paths enabled

### Styling & CSS

#### index.css - Design System
**Framework**: Tailwind CSS v4 with custom theme variables

**Custom Variant**:
- `dark` → class-based dark mode (`.dark` or `.dark *`)

**Theme Configuration** (inline):
- Animation: `--animate-skeleton` (2s infinite)
- 16 CSS custom properties for colors (primary, secondary, destructive, etc.)
- 7 radius variants (sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl)

**Color System** (Light theme):
```
Primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264) [Purple]
Background: white
Foreground: neutral-800
Secondary: black/4%
Muted: black/4%
```

**Dark Mode Overrides**:
- Background: neutral-950 95% + white
- Primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264) [Lighter purple]
- Borders & inputs: white/6-8% (vs black/8-10%)

**Font Stack**:
- Default: "DM Sans" (system fallback)
- Monospace: "SF Mono" / "SFMono-Regular" / Consolas / Menlo

**Scrollbar Styling**:
- Width: 6px
- Custom webkit styles for light/dark modes
- Track: transparent (always)
- Thumb: dark (15%) with hover (25%)

**Special Styling**:
- Electron drag region support (`.drag-region`, `-webkit-app-region`)
- Terminal drawer xterm scrollbar parity
- Chat markdown specific styles (word-break, overflow-wrap)
- No-transitions class for theme switching

#### Component System - CVA-based

**Button Component** (`button.tsx`):
- Uses CVA (class-variance-authority) for variants
- Variants:
  - `size`: default, icon, icon-lg, icon-sm, icon-xl, icon-xs, lg, sm, xl, xs
  - `variant`: default, destructive, destructive-outline, ghost, link, outline, secondary
- Responsive sizing with `sm:` breakpoints
- Built on Base UI's `useRender` hook
- Icon sizing auto-managed
- Touch target min-sizes (pointer-coarse)
- Focus ring indicators

**UI Components** (38+ in `/components/ui/`):
- `alert-dialog.tsx`, `alert.tsx`
- `autocomplete.tsx`, `combobox.tsx`, `command.tsx`
- `badge.tsx`, `button.tsx`, `card.tsx`
- `checkbox.tsx`, `radio-group.tsx`, `switch.tsx`, `toggle.tsx`, `toggle-group.tsx`
- `dialog.tsx`, `alert-dialog.tsx`, `sheet.tsx`
- `input.tsx`, `input-group.tsx`, `textarea.tsx`
- `select.tsx`, `menu.tsx`
- `popover.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`
- `sidebar.tsx` (collapsible offcanvas support)
- `scroll-area.tsx`, `separator.tsx`
- `skeleton.tsx`, `spinner.tsx`
- `field.tsx`, `fieldset.tsx`, `form.tsx`, `label.tsx`, `kbd.tsx`
- `empty.tsx`, `group.tsx`
- `toast.tsx` (with logic file)

### Source Directory Structure (`apps/web/src/`)

**Root Files**:
- `main.tsx` - Entry point (React StrictMode + RouterProvider)
- `router.ts` - Router setup with hash/browser history detection
- `App.tsx` - (referenced but main structure is routes-based)
- `types.ts` - Type definitions
- `env.ts` - Environment detection (isElectron)
- `branding.ts` - App display name
- `nativeApi.ts` - Native API bridge
- `index.css` - Global styles

**Store Files** (Zustand):
- `store.ts` - Main app store (projects, threads, models)
- `composerDraftStore.ts` - Composer draft persistence
- `terminalStateStore.ts` - Terminal state management
- `appSettings.ts` - App settings with localStorage persistence
- `sessionLogic.ts` - Session-related logic
- `session-logic.test.ts`

**Communication**:
- `wsNativeApi.ts` - WebSocket native API
- `wsTransport.ts` - WebSocket transport layer
- `nativeApi.ts` - Native API communication

**Logic Files** (Pure functions, well-tested):
- `composer-logic.ts` / `composer-editor-mentions.ts`
- `session-logic.ts`
- `keybindings.ts` - Keybinding management
- `history.ts` - History bootstrap logic
- `project-scripts.ts`
- `terminal-links.ts`
- `markdown-links.ts`
- `diff-route-search.ts`
- `pending-user-input.ts`
- `proposed-plan.ts`
- `chat-scroll.ts`
- `worktree-cleanup.ts`
- `vscode-icons.ts`

**Hooks** (`/hooks/`):
- `useTheme.ts` - Dark/light/system theme with localStorage
- `useMediaQuery.ts` - Media query listener
- `useTurnDiffSummaries.ts`

**Components** (`/components/`):
- `Sidebar.tsx` - Main navigation sidebar
- `ChatView.tsx` - Main chat interface (213KB - large component)
- `ChatView.browser.tsx` - Browser-specific chat view
- `ChatMarkdown.tsx` - Markdown rendering in chat
- `BranchToolbar.tsx` / `BranchToolbarBranchSelector.tsx` - Git branch selection
- `GitActionsControl.tsx` - Git control panel (30KB)
- `DiffPanel.tsx` - Diff visualization
- `ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx` - Terminal drawer
- `ComposerPromptEditor.tsx` - Editor for prompts
- `ProjectScriptsControl.tsx` - Project scripts UI
- `PlanSidebar.tsx` - Plan sidebar
- `Icons.tsx` - Custom icons
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` - Web worker pool for diffs
- `/ui/` - Reusable UI components (38+ components)

**Libraries** (`/lib/`):
- `diffRendering.ts` - Diff rendering utilities
- `gitReactQuery.ts` - React Query integration for git operations
- `providerReactQuery.ts` - React Query integration for provider operations
- `serverReactQuery.ts` - Server query configuration
- `lruCache.ts` - LRU cache implementation
- `projectReactQuery.ts` - Project queries
- `projectScriptKeybindings.ts` - Script keybinding utils
- `terminalStateCleanup.ts` - Terminal cleanup
- `turnDiffTree.ts` - Diff tree structures
- `utils.ts` - Utility functions
- `timelineHeight.ts` - Timeline height calculations

**Routes** (`/routes/` - TanStack Router):
- `__root.tsx` - Root layout with toast provider, error boundary
- `_chat.tsx` - Chat layout with sidebar
- `_chat.index.tsx` - Empty chat view
- `_chat.$threadId.tsx` - Individual thread view
- `_chat.settings.tsx` - Settings page
- `routeTree.gen.ts` - Auto-generated route tree

### Storage & Persistence

**localStorage Keys**:
- `t3code:theme` - Theme preference (light/dark/system)
- `t3code:renderer-state:v8` - App state (expanded projects, threads)
- `t3code:app-settings:v1` - App settings (model, service tier, custom models)

**Legacy keys** (migrated):
- `t3code:renderer-state:v7` through `v3`
- `codething:renderer-state:v4` through `v1`

### Hooks for Persistence

#### `useTheme()` - Theme Management
```typescript
const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme();
// theme: "light" | "dark" | "system"
// resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark" (computed)
// Features: localStorage sync, system preference detection, cross-tab sync
```

**Implementation**:
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state (localStorage + matchMedia)
- Detects system dark mode with `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
- Applies class `.dark` to document root
- Suppresses transitions during theme changes with `.no-transitions` class

#### `useAppSettings()` - Settings Management
- Stored in localStorage with validation via Effect schemas
- Max 32 custom models, max 256 chars per model slug
- Service tier: "auto", "fast", "flex"
- Codex binary and home paths (max 4096 chars)
- Streaming enabled/disabled flag

---

## 5. React Component Patterns

### Route Structure (TanStack Router)
```typescript
// Routes use createFileRoute or createRootRouteWithContext
export const Route = createFileRoute("/_chat/$threadId")({
  component: ThreadView,
  // Optional: head, params validation, etc.
});

// File naming: _segment.tsx creates layout, segment.tsx is route
// Dynamic: $param.tsx → route parameter
```

### Store Pattern (Zustand)

**Main Store** (`store.ts`):
```typescript
export interface AppState {
  projects: Project[];
  threads: Thread[];
  threadsHydrated: boolean;
}

// Persistence helpers:
readPersistedState(): AppState  // Read from localStorage
persistState(state): void       // Write to localStorage
```

**Features**:
- Pure helper functions for state updates
- Version-managed localStorage keys
- Legacy key cleanup on migration
- Error resilience (catches storage errors)

### Component Patterns

**Type-safe Props** via contracts:
```typescript
import { ThreadId, ProjectId } from "@t3tools/contracts";

interface ThreadProps {
  threadId: ThreadId;
  projectId: ProjectId;
}
```

**Responsive Tailwind Classes**:
```typescript
// sm: breakpoint for responsive design
className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-2 sm:gap-3"

// Dark mode support
className="bg-background text-foreground dark:bg-neutral-950"

// Touch targets
className="pointer-coarse:after:size-full pointer-coarse:after:min-h-11"
```

**Base UI Integration**:
```typescript
import { useRender } from "@base-ui/react/use-render";
import { mergeProps } from "@base-ui/react/merge-props";

function CustomButton({ render, ...props }: useRender.ComponentProps<"button">) {
  const merged = mergeProps(
    buttonVariants({ variant, size }),
    props
  );
  return useRender({ render, element: "button", ...merged });
}
```

## 7. TypeScript Configuration

### Base Config (`tsconfig.base.json`)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2023",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true,
    "noImplicitOverride": true,
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true
  }
}
```

**Strict mode** includes:
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` - Require index access bounds checking
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` - Strict optional property matching
- `noImplicitOverride` - Require override keyword for overridden methods

### App-specific Overrides (`apps/web/tsconfig.json`)
- `jsx`: "react-jsx"
- `lib`: ["ES2023", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]
- Path alias: `~/*` → `./src/*`
- Effect TypeScript LSP plugin for type safety

**T3 Code** is a sophisticated monorepo for AI-assisted code editing with:
- **Type-safe contracts** via Effect schemas (strict validation)

- **React 19 + Tailwind v4** for modern UI
- **TanStack Router** for file-based routing
- **Zustand** for lightweight state management

- **Comprehensive component library** with CVA variants
- **localStorage persistence** with versioned keys
- **Dark mode** with system preference detection
- **Electron compatibility** with hash routing and drag regions

# Custom Theme System for T3 Code Editor ## Context The app currently has a minimal two-state theme system (`"light" | "dark" | "system"`) stored at `t3code:theme` that only toggles a `.dark` class. The task is to introduce a fully-featured named theme system: three built-in themes with defined color tokens (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, status bar), a React ThemeProvider, an updated `useTheme` hook that applies CSS custom properties, and a visual ThemeSelector with preview swatches. Constraints: no build-config changes, no new dependencies. --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | Types + built-in theme data (no deps) | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | React wrapper component | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Visual swatch picker | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./theme"` subpath export | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Refactor for new ThemeName type + CSS var injection | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace radio list with `` | | `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | Wrap `RouterProvider` in `ThemeProvider` | --- ## Step-by-Step Implementation ### Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (new file) Pure TypeScript, zero external dependencies. ```typescript export type ThemeName = "t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"; export type ConcreteThemeName = Exclude; export interface ThemeColors { background: string; foreground: string; accent: string; accentForeground: string; sidebar: string; editor: string; terminal: string; statusBar: string; } export interface ThemeDefinition { name: ConcreteThemeName; label: string; description: string; isDark: boolean; colors: ThemeColors; } export const BUILT_IN_THEMES: Record = { "t3-light": { name: "t3-light", label: "Light", description: "Clean light theme with T3's signature purple.", isDark: false, colors: { background: "#ffffff", foreground: "#262626", accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "#f5f5f5", editor: "#ffffff", terminal: "#fafafa", statusBar: "#ebebeb", }, }, "t3-dark": { name: "t3-dark", label: "Dark", description: "Rich dark theme, easy on the eyes.", isDark: true, colors: { background: "color-mix(in srgb, #030712 95%, white)", foreground: "#f5f5f5", accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "color-mix(in srgb, #030712 97%, white)", editor: "#0a0a0a", terminal: "#0a0a0a", statusBar: "#0d0d0d", }, }, "t3-midnight": { name: "t3-midnight", label: "Midnight", description: "Deep blue-black with vibrant accents.", isDark: true, colors: { background: "oklch(0.10 0.025 264)", foreground: "#e8eaf6", accent: "oklch(0.65 0.25 280)", accentForeground: "#ffffff", sidebar: "oklch(0.09 0.03 264)", editor: "oklch(0.08 0.02 264)", terminal: "oklch(0.07 0.02 264)", statusBar: "oklch(0.09 0.03 264)", }, }, }; export const CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES: ReadonlyArray = [ "t3-light", "t3-dark", "t3-midnight", ]; ``` ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add one entry matching the existing pattern (types + import both point to the src file): ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (full rewrite) Key changes from the original 106-line file: - Import `ThemeName`, `ConcreteThemeName`, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` from `@t3tools/shared/theme` - New storage key: `"t3code:custom-theme"` (old: `"t3code:theme"`) - Add `migrateTheme()` that reads the new key first, then falls back to mapping the old key (`"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` / missing → `"system"`) - Replace `getStored()` with `migrateTheme()` everywhere - Update `applyTheme(theme: ThemeName)`: 1. Resolve concrete name (`system` → `t3-dark` or `t3-light` based on `getSystemDark()`) 2. Toggle `.dark` class from `ThemeDefinition.isDark` 3. Call `applyThemeCssVars(def.colors)` — sets the following CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement.style` using `setProperty`: - `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring` - `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar` 4. Keep the `no-transitions` / reflow / rAF suppression logic unchanged - **Do NOT** set `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` — these use Tailwind v4's `--alpha()` macro (build-time only) and auto-adapt via the `.dark` class - `--card` and `--popover` auto-update because their CSS definitions reference `var(--background)` - Preserve `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` return type (backward-compatible with any consumers that pass it to diff-rendering utilities): `BUILT_IN_THEMES[concreteName]?.isDark ? "dark" : "light"` - Storage event handler checks both `STORAGE_KEY` and the old key for cross-tab compatibility - The module-level `applyTheme(migrateTheme())` call on load is preserved for flash prevention `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` note: `BUILT_IN_THEMES[name]` infers `ThemeDefinition | undefined`. Guard with `if (def === undefined) return` in `applyTheme`, or use `BUILT_IN_THEMES[name] as ThemeDefinition` where the key is a `ConcreteThemeName` literal (following the existing pattern in `shared/src/model.ts` which uses `as` casts for known-exhaustive records). ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (new file) Thin component. Subscribes to theme changes so the internal `useEffect` in `useTheme` runs from the top of the tree (important for StrictMode safety and SSR hydration order): ```tsx import { type ReactNode } from "react"; import { useTheme } from "~/hooks/useTheme"; export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children?: ReactNode }) { // Subscribing here ensures useTheme's internal useEffect fires at the root, // keeping CSS vars and .dark in sync through React's lifecycle (StrictMode safe). useTheme(); return <>{children}; } ``` The original `useTheme` already contains `useEffect(() => { applyTheme(theme); }, [theme])`. Mounting `ThemeProvider` at the root means this effect runs before any child renders needing the theme. `applyTheme` is idempotent so StrictMode double-invocation is harmless. ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) Renders one button per theme (3 concrete + 1 system). Preview boxes use **inline styles with hard-coded color values** from `BUILT_IN_THEMES` — NOT `var(--*)` — so swatches show correct colors independent of the currently active theme. Preview swatch anatomy (64×40px box): - Full-width background fill - Left strip ~12px: sidebar color - Bottom strip ~6px: statusBar color - Small circle top-right: accent color System swatch: left half uses t3-light background, right half uses t3-dark background. Follows existing styling patterns from `_chat.settings.tsx`: - Selected state: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` + "Active" badge (`bg-primary/14 text-primary`) - Unselected: `border-border bg-background hover:bg-accent text-muted-foreground` - `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` for accessibility - Uses `cn()` from `~/lib/utils` Import chain: `@t3tools/shared/theme` (BUILT_IN_THEMES, CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES, ThemeName), `~/hooks/useTheme`, `~/lib/utils` (cn). ### Step 6 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant (lines ~25–41) - Remove `theme` and `setTheme` from `useTheme()` destructure (only `resolvedTheme` stays) - Remove the `
…
` block (~lines 210–238) - Add import: `import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector";` - Replace removed block with `` - Keep the "Active theme: {resolvedTheme}" paragraph unchanged ### Step 7 — `apps/web/src/main.tsx` ```tsx import { ThemeProvider } from "./components/ThemeProvider"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( , ); ``` --- ## Architecture Notes **Why only 5 CSS vars are overridden (not all 20):** `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input` use `--alpha(var(--color-black) / N%)` which is a Tailwind v4 build-time CSS macro — it cannot be reproduced via `setProperty`. These variables auto-adapt to dark/light via the `.dark` class, which we control. `--card` and `--popover` use `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` and cascade automatically once `--background` is overridden from JS. **Why `resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"`:** This return type is relied upon by diff-rendering logic (e.g. any component passing `resolvedTheme` to `@pierre/diffs` theme configuration). Keeping it `"light" | "dark"` avoids breaking changes. **Why no `@theme inline` additions in `index.css`:** The 4 new `--theme-*` variables are set entirely from JS. They are usable anywhere via `var(--theme-sidebar)` in inline styles or Tailwind's arbitrary-value syntax `[var(--theme-sidebar)]` without being registered in `@theme inline`. No CSS file changes needed. --- ## Verification ```bash # Must both pass before done bun typecheck # from packages/shared and apps/web bun lint # from apps/web ``` **Manual testing checklist:** 1. Fresh load → no theme flash; correct theme applied 2. Switch to each of the 3 concrete themes → `document.documentElement.style` has correct `--background`, `--primary`, `--theme-sidebar`, etc.; `.dark` class present/absent correctly 3. Switch to "system" → follows OS preference; switching OS preference updates theme live 4. Reload page → selected theme persists 5. Migration: set `localStorage["t3code:theme"] = "dark"`, clear `t3code:custom-theme`, reload → migrates to `t3-dark` automatically 6. Open two tabs → changing theme in one tab updates the other (storage event) 7. ThemeSelector swatches → each swatch shows its own colors even when a different theme is active 8. `resolvedTheme` text in Settings → shows "light" or "dark" correctly 9. `--card` / `--popover` → verify they track `--background` changes (inspect in DevTools) 10. `--secondary` / `--border` → verify they are NOT overridden by JS (only `.dark` class affects them)

content.text:
All 7 steps are complete. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was implemented

### New files

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — The shared type definition with zero dependencies:
- `ThemeName` = `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"`
- `ConcreteThemeName` = `Exclude<ThemeName, "system">`
- `ThemeColors` interface covering all 7 areas: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `accentForeground`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar`
- `ThemeDefinition` interface with `name`, `label`, `description`, `isDark`, `colors`
- `BUILT_IN_THEMES` record with all three concrete themes
- `CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES` ordered array for iteration

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React wrapper that subscribes to the theme store at the root of the tree, ensuring the `useEffect` in `useTheme` fires before any child renders.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Visual swatch picker with:
- One button per theme (Light, Dark, Midnight + System)
- Mini preview boxes using **hard-coded inline style colors** (not CSS vars), so swatches show correct colors regardless of active theme — sidebar strip on left, status bar strip on bottom, accent dot in corner
- System option shows a split half-light/half-dark preview
- Accessible `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` markup
- Selected state with "Active" badge, matching the existing settings page style

### Modified files

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export following the existing pattern.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite:
- Imports types and data from `@t3tools/shared/theme`
- New storage key `t3code:custom-theme` with automatic **migration** of the old `t3code:theme` key (`dark`→`t3-dark`, `light`→`t3-light`)
- `applyTheme()` now sets both the `.dark` class **and** injects 9 CSS custom properties: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring`, `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar`
- `resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"` for backward compatibility
- Cross-tab sync watches both old and new storage keys

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Removed `THEME_OPTIONS` array and the manual radio-button list; replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`.

**`apps/web/src/main.tsx`** — Wrapped `RouterProvider` in `<ThemeProvider>`.

All 7 steps are complete. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was implemented

### New files

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — The shared type definition with zero dependencies:
- `ThemeName` = `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight" | "system"`
- `ConcreteThemeName` = `Exclude<ThemeName, "system">`
- `ThemeColors` interface covering all 7 areas: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `accentForeground`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar`
- `ThemeDefinition` interface with `name`, `label`, `description`, `isDark`, `colors`
- `BUILT_IN_THEMES` record with all three concrete themes
- `CONCRETE_THEME_NAMES` ordered array for iteration

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React wrapper that subscribes to the theme store at the root of the tree, ensuring the `useEffect` in `useTheme` fires before any child renders.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Visual swatch picker with:
- One button per theme (Light, Dark, Midnight + System)
- Mini preview boxes using **hard-coded inline style colors** (not CSS vars), so swatches show correct colors regardless of active theme — sidebar strip on left, status bar strip on bottom, accent dot in corner
- System option shows a split half-light/half-dark preview
- Accessible `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` markup
- Selected state with "Active" badge, matching the existing settings page style

### Modified files

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export following the existing pattern.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite:
- Imports types and data from `@t3tools/shared/theme`
- New storage key `t3code:custom-theme` with automatic **migration** of the old `t3code:theme` key (`dark`→`t3-dark`, `light`→`t3-light`)
- `applyTheme()` now sets both the `.dark` class **and** injects 9 CSS custom properties: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--ring`, `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar`
- `resolvedTheme` stays `"light" | "dark"` for backward compatibility
- Cross-tab sync watches both old and new storage keys

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Removed `THEME_OPTIONS` array and the manual radio-button list; replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`.

**`apps/web/src/main.tsx`** — Wrapped `RouterProvider` in `<ThemeProvider>`.
